title: Safe Use of Banking Apps
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you verify that a banking app is legitimate?
What additional security measures should you implement?

stages.prompt: You install a banking app. What should you check first?

options.label: App permissions and official source
options.reflection: Exactly! Verifying the app is from an official source and checking its permissions helps protect your data.

options.label: Nothing
options.reflection: Installing a banking app without verification could expose your financial data to security risks.

options.label: Just the app store rating
options.reflection: While ratings are helpful, they don't guarantee security. Checking official sources is more important.

options.label: Download size of the app
options.reflection: App size doesn't indicate security. Verifying the official source and permissions is more important.

stages.prompt: What is the safest way to download a banking app?

options.label: From third-party app stores or websites
options.reflection: Third-party sources may host malicious versions of banking apps that steal your data.

options.label: From links in emails or messages
options.reflection: Links in emails or messages may lead to fake apps designed to steal your banking credentials.

options.label: From general search engines
options.reflection: Search engines may direct you to fake apps. Always use official app stores or bank websites.

options.label: From the official app store or bank's website
options.reflection: Exactly! Official sources provide authentic apps with security measures in place.

stages.prompt: When using a banking app, which practice is safest?

options.label: Using it on public Wi-Fi networks
options.reflection: Public Wi-Fi is insecure and could allow hackers to intercept your banking data.

options.label: Using it anywhere with any internet connection
options.reflection: Not all connections are secure. Using banking apps on private, secured networks is safer.

options.label: Using it on your private, secured network
options.reflection: Exactly! Private, secured networks provide better protection for your banking transactions.

options.label: On shared or borrowed devices
options.reflection: Shared devices may have malware or store your login information insecurely.

stages.prompt: What should you do to protect your banking app account?

options.label: Use a simple password that's easy to remember
options.reflection: Simple passwords are easier to crack. Strong, unique passwords provide better security.

options.label: Use strong passwords and two-factor authentication
options.reflection: Exactly! Strong passwords and 2FA significantly enhance the security of your banking account.

options.label: Reuse the same password across multiple apps
options.reflection: Password reuse increases risk. If one account is compromised, others become vulnerable.

options.label: Write down your password in a notebook
options.reflection: Writing passwords down creates physical security risks. Digital password managers are safer.

stages.prompt: How often should you log out of your banking app?

options.label: Regularly, especially when using shared devices
options.reflection: Exactly! Regular logout helps protect your account from unauthorized access.

options.label: Never log out, just put the app in background
options.reflection: Leaving banking apps logged in increases the risk if your device is accessed by others.

options.label: Only when you remember to
options.reflection: Making logout routine rather than occasional provides more consistent security.

options.label: Rarely, since the app has automatic security
options.reflection: While apps have security features, manual logout adds an extra layer of protection.
